title: Midnight at the Museum
author: Isla Reyes
pages:
Chapter 1: The New Exhibit

Dr. Prachi adjusted her thick-rimmed glasses, holding her breath to prevent even a microscopic drop of condensation from touching the 16th-century golden chalice she was cleaning. A fine camel-hair brush swept absolute stillness over the engravings. At 2:00 AM, the Metropolitan Museum of Art was her absolute domain. She had ignored three successive vibrations from her phone on the desk; the ancient relics on her table offered a structure of absolute safety and quiet that the outer, living schedule rarely provided.

Footsteps echoed sharply against the marble floor of the Medieval Hall. Heavy, deliberate, and undeniably human. Prachi froze, the brush pausing mid-stroke.

'You realize the museum is closed, Doc?' a smooth, slightly gruff voice called out from the shadows.

A figure stepped into the dim security lighting. It was Ashish, the new night shift security guard. He was tall, athletic, with a sharp jawline and an infuriatingly relaxed posture. He looked more like a restless former mercenary than a guy guarding old pottery. Prachi found him both distracting and highly annoying, mostly because he always seemed to catch her talking to the artifacts.

'Yes, Mr. Soni. I am well aware,' Prachi said primly, setting the brush down. 'But the new Renaissance exhibit opens in three days, and the cataloging is behind schedule. The artifacts require delicate handling, unlike the blunt force you employ on the staff breakroom vending machine.'

Ashish chuckled, leaning casually against a stone pillar. 'The vending machine stole my dollar, Doc. It was a matter of principle. Besides, you look like you haven't slept since Tuesday. Go home. The shiny cups will be here tomorrow.'

Chapter 2: The Missing Piece

Prachi ignored him, returning to her work. But when she arrived the next morning, chaos reigned. Alarm bells were ringing, and police tape crisscrossed the Medieval Hall. The centerpiece of the new exhibit—a priceless, jewel-encrusted dagger—was gone. And the only person on duty in that sector the night before was Ashish Soni.

Prachi pushed through the crowd of detectives, her heart hammering. Ashish was sitting on a bench near the entrance, looking remarkably unbothered while a detective peppered him with questions.

'Dr. Soni,' the lead detective barked, catching her eye. 'You were the last one to log out of the system near the vault room last night. Anything unusual?'

'Nothing,' she said, her eyes flashing to Ashish. 'Everything was secure when I left at 3:00 AM. Mr. Thorne was on patrol.'

'Yeah, and I saw nothing either,' Ashish sighed, running a hand through his hair. 'Cameras went dark for a three-minute loop at 4:15 AM. A professional job. Ghosted right past me.'

Prachi didn't believe him. Not completely. He was too calm. Too collected. That night, she returned to the museum after hours, determined to figure out what happened to her beloved artifact. She pulled up the security logs in the server room, tracking the system anomalies.

'You're entirely too curious for your own good, Doc,' a voice whispered right behind her ear.

Prachi gasped, spinning around. Ashish was leaning against the server rack, arms crossed. He didn't look like a bored security guard anymore; he looked dangerous. The shadows clung to him differently.

Chapter 3: The Inside Job

'You took it,' Prachi realized, her voice trembling slightly. 'Why? Money? You don't strike me as a common thief.'

Ashish stepped closer, entirely crowding her space in the narrow aisle. The scent of clean linen and something sharply metallic wafted off him. 'I didn't steal it, Prachi. I secured it. Before the actual thieves could grab it on their way out.'

'What do you mean?'

'I mean, the museum curator has a gambling debt the size of a small yacht. He contracted an art ring to lift the dagger using the camera loop to collect the insurance money. I intercepted it.'

Prachi stared at him, her analytical brain spinning. 'And you expect me to believe you're a heroic, rogue security guard playing vigilante?'

Ashish smirked, the dangerous edge softening. 'I'm an undercover investigator for the Art Loss Register, Doc. And right now, I have a priceless dagger locked in my apartment safe, and I need a certified expert to help me prove its authenticity to expose the curator before he pins the theft on me.'

Chapter 4: Undercover Operation

Prachi found herself hopelessly swept into Ashish's world. Her quiet, organized life shattered. She spent the next two days practically living out of Ashish's sparse downtown apartment, hunched over his kitchen table, analyzing the intricate filigree of the dagger under a magnifying lamp while he paced nervously.

He cooked her dinner. He made sure she drank coffee. He watched her work with an intensity that made her skin prickle. Beneath the gruff, mercenary exterior, she discovered a surprisingly sharp intellect and a fierce protective streak.

'You have incredible focus,' Ashish noted quietly one evening, watching her meticulously document the blade's wear patterns. He leaned over her chair, his chest brushing her shoulder. 'It's mesmerizing.'

Prachi's breath hitched, the magnifying glass slipping slightly in her hand. She looked up, their faces inches apart. The air between them crackled, fraught with a tension entirely unrelated to stolen antiquities.

'And you have an annoying habit of distracting me, Ashish,' she whispered, not moving an inch.

He didn't smirk this time. He just leaned in, capturing her lips with a slow, searing kiss that sent a jolt straight to Prachi's core. It was demanding, intense, and completely broke down every wall she had ever built around herself.

Chapter 5: The Sting

The sting operation went down at a high-end black market auction. Prachi, dressed in an uncharacteristically glamorous, plunging emerald gown, acted as the reluctant buyer, while Ashish played her imposing bodyguard. They cornered the curator and the art ring leaders in a private viewing room.

When the police burst in, chaos erupted. A thug lunged at Prachi, but Ashish was there in a flash, tackling the man to the ground with terrifying efficiency.

As the sirens wailed and the curator was hauled away in handcuffs, Ashish stood up, brushing off his tailored suit. He walked straight over to Prachi, ignoring the pandemonium around them.

'You played the part of the wealthy socialite perfectly, Doc,' he breathed, wrapping his arms around her waist tightly. 'Terrifyingly well.'

Prachi smiled, looping her arms around his neck. The adrenaline of the sting faded, replaced by the profound, dizzying realization that she didn't want him to ever let go.

'I suppose I had a good bodyguard to keep me focused,' she replied, kissing him deeply against the backdrop of flashing police lights. The quiet life of an art historian was officially over, and she couldn't be happier.
